name: Kuala Lumpur
lifestyle: Warm tropical capital with strong English fluency, affordable premium housing in Bangsar/Mont Kiara, and modern healthcare at a fraction of Western costs. International school tuition and domestic help are the swing costs. MM2H (Malaysia My Second Home) is the established Fat FIRE residency route.

true_fat_fire.description: KLCC/Mont Kiara 2,500+ sqft condo with pool and gym, live-in helper plus part-time driver. Fine dining weekly, weekend Bali/Phuket trips, premium private healthcare.

guide.groceries: Village Grocer and imported-food baskets plus fresh-market top-ups: premium fruit, Australian beef, seafood, wine, and household staples without much price sensitivity.
guide.dining: Several polished meals out each week, hotel brunches, and regular chef-driven dinners, with nicer nights often around RM250-600 per person.
guide.rhythm: Grab or driver-led mobility, club or spa spending, easy household staffing, and a travel pattern that treats regional luxury weekends as routine.

comfortable_expat.description: Mont Kiara/Bangsar mid-rise 2BR condo. Casual dining nightly, part-time helper 2-3 days/week, 1-2 regional trips plus annual Europe/US visit.

guide.groceries: Expat-friendly supermarkets plus wet-market top-ups: imported dairy and pantry staples, solid produce, and a comfortable but not extravagant at-home kitchen.
guide.dining: Casual dining 2-3 times a week and a nicer dinner every few weeks, usually around RM40-100 per person casually and RM180-350 on a bigger night.
guide.rhythm: Mostly Grab, moderate wellness or grooming, occasional help for cleaning, and a lifestyle that leans into regional convenience rather than nonstop luxury.

luxury_family.description: Mont Kiara villa or low-rise 3-4BR with pool, live-in helper plus driver. Top international school access (ISKL / Alice Smith) when dependents apply, regional weekend trips plus annual long-haul.

guide.groceries: Large family basket across premium grocers and bulk runs: imported snacks, good fruit, higher-end proteins, school provisioning, and easy entertaining at home.
guide.dining: Family brunches or dinners most weekends plus adult meals out, with relaxed family meals often RM60-140 per person and nicer venues far above that.
guide.rhythm: Live-in household help, driver coverage when needed, club or activity spending for children, and frequent short-haul breaks around Southeast Asia.
